notes: Observed with Qwen3-Coder-30B on local deployment (73K context, vLLM). Double-escaped sequences are the most frequent issue.

entries.name: Double-escaped newline
entries.description: In Python source code, '\n' inside a string literal produces the two characters backslash+n, not a newline. Use a single backslash if you want a real newline.

entries.name: Double-escaped tab
entries.description: In Python source code, '\t' inside a string literal produces the two characters backslash+t, not a tab. Use a single backslash if you want a real tab.

entries.name: Double-escaped quote
entries.description: In Python source code, '\"' inside a double-quoted string literal produces the two characters backslash+quote, not an escaped quote.

entries.name: Double-escaped backslash in f-string/join
entries.description: '\n' or '\t' in an f-string or regular string produces literal backslash sequences instead of escapes.

entries.name: F-string with escaped braces
entries.description: Weak models sometimes write f'{{var}}' thinking it inserts var. In f-strings {{ }} are literal braces; use {var} for interpolation.

entries.name: asyncio.run inside async function
entries.description: asyncio.run() cannot be called from a running event loop (e.g. inside another async function). Use 'await' directly instead.
